summary: Derived valuation metrics from Polymarket 'closing market cap above $X' YES prices. Each 'above $X' market is a separate order book, so raw midpoints are arbitrage-adjusted (volume-weighted isotonic regression) into a monotone CDF before any metric is computed. Raw values are preserved alongside the adjusted ones. Two output tiers are kept strictly separate: TIER 1 (derived.market) are pure market transforms with no assumptions; TIER 2 (derived.scenarios) require external inputs and each carries a sourced, dated, ranged assumption (see assumptions.json). A scenario with no usable assumption renders 'input_required', never a fabricated number.

isotonic_adjustment.method: Volume-weighted Pool Adjacent Violators (PAVA) enforces a non-increasing CDF across thresholds. On a violation (an earlier P(>X) below a later one), adjacent markets pool to their volume-weighted mean, pulling the estimate toward the more liquid quote. Each market stores raw_prob and adjusted_prob; all metrics use the adjusted curve.
assumptions: Weight = market volume (fallback 1 when volume is unavailable, e.g. price-only history).
isotonic_adjustment.sensitivity_note: Removes negative-probability artifacts that arise because the markets are not jointly arbitrage-constrained. Per-snapshot monotonicity_violations and max_adjustment are published.

implied_median.method: Threshold where the adjusted CDF P(cap > X) crosses 0.50, by linear interpolation.
assumptions: Monotone adjusted survival curve (guaranteed by isotonic adjustment).
implied_median.sensitivity_note: Robust: depends only on the two thresholds straddling 50%.

median_band.method: Spread-implied uncertainty on the median (live only): the best_bid and best_ask curves are each isotonic-adjusted, each one's 0.50 crossing is taken, and the median is bounded by { low: min, high: max } of the two. null when no two-sided book exists (price-only history).
assumptions: Bid/ask quotes are present for enough thresholds to cross 0.50; missing quotes are excluded, never synthesized.
median_band.sensitivity_note: Converts the point median into a defensible range; width reflects book tightness.

implied_mean.method: Bucket-weighted expected value over the adjusted distribution; tails use midpoint offsets (base case: lowest - 0.15, highest + 0.40).
assumptions: Below-lowest midpoint = lowest - 0.15 ($T).
Above-highest midpoint = highest + 0.40 ($T).
Within-bucket mass at the bucket midpoint.
implied_mean.sensitivity_note: Assumption-sensitive; a sensitivity range is published (see mean_sensitivity).

mean_sensitivity.method: implied_mean evaluated over a 3x3 grid of tail midpoints (below in {0.10,0.15,0.20}, above in {0.30,0.40,0.60}); reported as { central (base case), low (grid min), high (grid max) }.
assumptions: The grid spans plausible tail placements.
mean_sensitivity.sensitivity_note: A tight band proves the mean is robust to tail assumptions; a wide band discloses fragility.

iqr.method: p25/p75 valuations = adjusted CDF crossings at 0.75 and 0.25 (the 50% confidence band).
assumptions: Monotone adjusted survival curve.
iqr.sensitivity_note: Robust interpolation; null when a bound lies outside the quoted range.

bucket_prob.method: adjusted P(> this) - adjusted P(> next); top bucket = adjusted P(> top); a '< lowest' bucket = 1 - adjusted P(> lowest) completes the distribution.
assumptions: Computed from the adjusted (monotone) curve, so every bucket is >= 0 and all buckets sum to 1.0 (asserted at build).
bucket_prob.sensitivity_note: No modelling assumptions beyond the isotonic adjustment.

confidence.method: Worst-of several signals, each producing a human-readable reason: active-threshold count, monotonicity adjustments (count + max magnitude), mean bid/ask spread, thin-liquidity breadth (share of markets below a volume floor), and anomalies (inputs identical to prior snapshot, markets closed/not accepting orders, total volume far below the trailing 7-day median). Tier is capped at 'medium' for price-only history.
assumptions: high: >=12 thresholds AND 0 adjustments AND mean spread < 0.04 AND deep books AND no anomalies.
medium: 8-11 thresholds, OR <=2 adjustments, OR spread 0.04-0.08, OR 20-50% thin, OR price-only, OR a flagged anomaly.
low: <8 thresholds, OR >2 adjustments, OR spread > 0.08, OR >50% thin, OR multiple closed markets.
confidence.sensitivity_note: Liquidity breadth is not assessed when volume is unknown (price-only history).

raw_sha256.method: sha256 over the canonical JSON serialization of raw_inputs (fixed key order: token_id, threshold, midpoint, best_bid, best_ask, volume; ascending by threshold; literal API string values). Unchanged from 1.0.0 so archived snapshots remain verifiable.
assumptions: Consumers re-serialize the stored raw_inputs identically to reproduce the hash.
raw_sha256.sensitivity_note: Provenance check: proves derived numbers came from the stored, unaltered inputs. Status flags (closed/active) are deliberately excluded from the hashed inputs.

categorical.method: A categorical event (named mutually-exclusive outcomes, e.g. 'How many Fed rate cuts in 2026?') is N Yes/No legs whose YES midpoints form a PMF. Each outcome's displayed probability is the leg's YES midpoint NORMALIZED so the distribution sums to 1 (de-vig: the market-maker overround is removed). Consensus = normalized Shannon entropy over the outcomes (0 = one outcome certain, 1 = uniform). Dominant outcome = the largest normalized probability.
assumptions: Legs are mutually exclusive and collectively exhaustive (a valid PMF).
categorical.sensitivity_note: HASH RECIPE (same as every kind): raw_sha256 is over the RAW observed YES midpoints in raw_inputs (synthetic threshold = leg index for a stable canonical sort), NOT the normalized probabilities. Normalization is a display transform only — the hash is over truth, not presentation.

narrative.method: Deterministic, template-based reading composed only from stored fields (median vs 7d/30d-ago, divergence, dominant bucket, shape, dispersion trend, confidence caveat). No language model in the data path; reproducible from narrative_components. Trend claims are suppressed on low-confidence days.
assumptions: Every clause maps to a field in narrative_components; nothing is asserted that is not stored.
narrative.sensitivity_note: Same inputs always produce the same sentence.

analytics_shape.method: Distribution shape from the adjusted CDF: Bowley quartile skewness ((p75-med)-(med-p25))/(p75-p25); a robust fat-tail ratio (10th-90th percentile valuation spread) / IQR vs a normal's 1.90; normalized Shannon entropy and Gini over the density buckets (consensus vs dispersion); dominant bucket.
assumptions: Quantile- and probability-based only; no tail-midpoint assumptions.
analytics_shape.sensitivity_note: Bowley skew and the tail ratio use only interpolated quantiles, so they inherit none of the mean's tail sensitivity.

analytics_dispersion.method: The 25-75% band width (p75-p25) tracked over time; trend = converging when the width is shrinking (growing certainty), diverging when widening.
assumptions: Reads stored historical IQR; no assumptions.
analytics_dispersion.sensitivity_note: A genuine signal: a tightening band means the market is concentrating its view.

analytics_velocity.method: Rate of change of the implied median: 24h/7d/30d changes (the single source for displayed median deltas), 7d/30d annualized drift, and acceleration from the sign of the second difference.
assumptions: Reads stored historical medians; no assumptions.
analytics_velocity.sensitivity_note: All surfaces display the same stored delta values through one rounding helper.

calibration.method: Structural scaffold only. The market resolves once, at IPO close (2027-12-31), so a true calibration / Brier score is impossible before resolution. The standing forecast is recorded so it can be scored later; NO score is computed now.
assumptions: None — explicitly pending resolution.

scenarios.method: TIER 2. Implied share price = market_cap / shares_outstanding, reported as {central, low, high} from the shares range; round-over-round = implied IPO-close median vs the last reported valuation. Each scenario carries its sourced, dated assumption from assumptions.json; with no usable input it renders 'input_required'.
assumptions: shares_outstanding and last_round_valuation are external, press-estimated, low-confidence inputs (SpaceX is private). See assumptions.json and the embedded per-scenario assumptions.
scenarios.sensitivity_note: Quarantined from Tier 1 and validated: no scenario number exists without a sourced+dated assumption; no assumption leaks into derived.market. The published feed uses the registry value; the dashboard lets a user explore their own without mutating the feed.

source_of_record.method: The canonical price is the CLOB /midpoints value = (best_bid + best_ask) / 2 of the live order book. raw_inputs.midpoint (hence every raw_prob and all derived metrics) is this CLOB midpoint. Gamma outcomePrices is a platform-surfaced display price that can lag the book; it is used ONLY for metadata (token ids, volume, threshold) and as a cross-check, never as an input. On disagreement the CLOB midpoint is authoritative and the divergence is reported.
assumptions: None — both inputs are public, auth-free Polymarket endpoints.
source_of_record.sensitivity_note: Accuracy is independently proven by scripts/verify-accuracy.js: dual-source fetch twice, cross-source agreement (±1pt), drift quantification, published-vs-live reconciliation (±2pt + drift) with a separate freshness verdict, and isotonic-validity of the live curve. Report-only; tolerances documented, not tuned to pass.

freshness.method: TIER 1. derived.freshness is a pure function of the snapshot's own fetched_at plus one documented constant (no external input): { as_of, staleness_threshold_hours, stale_after = as_of + threshold, expected_cadence, policy }. The published record carries POLICY ONLY — no frozen age/stale flag, since age is a read-time quantity. Consumers (the dashboard and note) evaluate staleness with a single comparison, now > stale_after, and display an as-of age + stale badge. core/freshness.js is the single source of the policy.
assumptions: None — derived solely from the snapshot's own timestamp.
freshness.sensitivity_note: Threshold = 17h, DERIVED from the snapshot schedule rather than chosen as a literal: the cron (update.yml, '0 0,12,14,16,18,20,22 * * *') runs every 2h 12:00-22:00 UTC plus 00:00, daily incl. weekends, so the largest scheduled gap is the 12h overnight pause (00:00->12:00 UTC). Threshold = 12h max gap + 2h one fully-missed run + 3h Actions queue jitter. A normal overnight pause never flags; anything past 17h is a genuine pipeline failure. core/freshness.js exports the SCHEDULE facts and computes the threshold from them; test/schedule-coupling.test.js re-derives the gaps from the workflow cron so schedule and threshold cannot silently desync. The weekday-only crons are the email runs, not the snapshot.

changelog.change: Consensus/decisiveness → RELIABILITY (Increment B), additive (no schema change; confidence shape unchanged). CATEGORICAL: a strongly-concentrated distribution (normalized entropy ≤ 0.40 AND a ≥70% leader) LIFTS a spread-driven 'medium' reliability to 'high' — the headline outcome is a well-formed agreement, reliable even when long-tail legs have wider books (the mean-spread averaging that depressed it isn't what the headline rests on). It never overrides a genuine defect: a >8% averaged book stays 'low', and a missing/last-trade outcome still caps. BINARY: a decisive probability (≤0.02 or ≥0.98) whose spread is a MINORITY of the smaller tail (≤50%) adds a 'price well-determined' reliability basis; there is NO tier lift for binary (a single book has no wide-tail-leg averaging to correct, and a medium-band spread on an extreme line always dominates the tiny tail). Sharpens the CT-Governor case: 98% consensus now reads reliability HIGH explicitly, independent of its (low) liquidity. Ladder/touch unchanged; SpaceX parity 4/4 byte-identical (categorical/binary-only).

Confidence SPLIT into two independent dimensions (Increment A), NO change to the underlying signals/thresholds — they are REPARTITIONED, not retuned. derived.confidence is now { reliability, liquidity }, each a {tier,score,reasons}. RELIABILITY (is the displayed number trustworthy) owns: threshold count, monotonicity, spread (+expiry tolerance), last-trade fallback, missing rungs, stale feed. LIQUIDITY (can you transact at this price) owns: book-thin breadth, windowed volume (all-time fallback), closed/not-accepting-orders rungs, liquidity drop. The two are orthogonal — a market can be HIGH reliability + LOW liquidity (strong consensus, nobody trading) or the reverse. The old single tier is recoverable as worst(reliability, liquidity). Schema 2.0.0 (BREAKING: confidence shape). SpaceX frozen output: reliability HIGH + liquidity HIGH; the parity gate's faithfulness proof asserts reliability.tier === the frozen single tier, worst(reliability,liquidity) === it, and the two reason lists' union === the frozen reasons.

Multi-market generalization (Phase 1), NO formula change. Every market-specific value (tail offsets, mean-sensitivity grid, liquidity floor, confidence count thresholds, dispersion/accel eps, density label unit, tracked thresholds, narrative wording, calibration date, asset metadata) moved into a per-market MarketConfig (core/markets/*.json); the pipeline now processes any threshold-ladder event, not just SpaceX. SpaceX's config pins the exact prior constants, so its output is byte-identical (proven by a blocking parity gate: raw_sha256 + full derived block + 183-day history). Added a two-stage market-resolution lifecycle (snapshot.lifecycle: OPEN / CLOSED_PENDING / RESOLVED) — a resolved market is frozen and never re-pulled, a closed-but-unconfirmed market claims no final outcome, and a non-OPEN market is final (not flagged stale). Tier-2 scenarios are now optional (attached only when a market registers an assumptions file); the firewall is unchanged. Schema 1.3.0 (additive: snapshot.lifecycle; derived.scenarios optional; assumptions_version nullable).

Cadence + freshness policy change, NO formula change. Snapshot cadence raised from 1/day (14:00 UTC) to every 2h 12:00-00:00 UTC (7/day, overnight pause 00:00-12:00 UTC); email crons unchanged. Staleness threshold re-derived as a function of the schedule (12h max scheduled gap + 2h one missed run + 3h queue jitter = 17h, was a 50h literal sized for the daily cadence) — core/freshness.js now exports the SCHEDULE facts and computes the threshold from them, with a coupling test re-deriving the gaps from the workflow cron. Added a post-publish accuracy verify step in CI (publish-then-alert: scripts/verify-accuracy.js runs non-strict after every push; a FAIL marks the run red but never blocks publication or emails). Minor bump: published policy fields (staleness_threshold_hours, expected_cadence, policy) change meaning for consumers; no metric formula or schema shape changed.

Clarification + additive freshness field, NO formula change. Documented the canonical source of record (CLOB /midpoints; Gamma outcomePrices is a lagging cross-check, never an input). Added scripts/verify-accuracy.js, an independent data-accuracy harness that reconciles the published feed against fresh dual-source data with documented tolerances and a freshness verdict (report-only). Added Tier-1 derived.freshness (as-of anchor + absolute stale_after; 50h threshold sized to the daily cron) surfaced as an as-of age + stale badge on the dashboard and note. Schema 1.2.1 (additive: derived.freshness). No change to any published metric value.

Added Tier-1 market analytics (Bowley skew, robust fat-tail ratio, entropy/Gini, dispersion-over-time, velocity/acceleration) and a calibration scaffold (pending resolution, not faked). Added a firewalled Tier-2 scenario tier (derived.scenarios: implied share price, round-over-round) where every number carries a sourced, dated, ranged assumption (assumptions.json v1.0.0); validation fails the build on any unsourced scenario or Tier-2 leak. Unified delta rounding (fixes a narrative/card mismatch). Schema 1.2.0 (additive: derived.market, derived.scenarios, assumptions_version).

bucket_prob/CDF now volume-weighted isotonic-adjusted (removes negative-probability artifacts; raw preserved alongside adjusted); added spread-propagated median uncertainty band (live only); added mean tail-sensitivity range; added anomaly/staleness/liquidity signals to confidence; added deterministic narrative; published JSON Schema (schema.json) validated at build.
Initial published methodology: median, mean (with tail assumptions), IQR band, bucket probabilities, three-signal confidence, and raw_sha256 provenance.
